---
title: "Version_withG3_outliers"
author: "HONGJIE_DENG"
date: "2025-10-21"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggfortify)
math_final_grade <- read_csv2("student/student-mat.csv")
glimpse(math_final_grade)
```


```{r}
library(caret)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)


df_long <- math_final_grade %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(G3, G1, G2, absences, failures, famrel),
               names_to = "variable",
               values_to = "value")


ggplot(df_long, aes(x = value, fill = variable)) +
  geom_histogram(color = "white", bins = 20, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ variable, scales = "free", ncol = 3) +
  labs(title = "Histograms of G1, G2, Absences, Failures, and Famrel",
       x = "Value", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11)
  )

```


```{r}
hist(math_final_grade$G3, breaks = 21, main = "G3 distribution")
```
```{r}
sum(math_final_grade$G3 <= 10)
```




```{r}
#This is the backwards stepwise selection for the full model
step.back.aic <- step(
  lm(G3 ~ ., data = math_final_grade), 
  direction = "backward",
  trace = FALSE
)
round(summary(step.back.aic)$coef, 3)
```

```{r}
model0 <- lm(G3 ~ G1 + G2 + absences + famrel + activities + age, data=math_final_grade) 
autoplot(model0, which = 1:2, ncol = 2)
```
```{r}
model0.1 <- lm(sqrt(G3) ~ G1 + G2 + absences + famrel + activities + age, data=math_final_grade) 
autoplot(model0.1, which = 1:2, ncol = 2)
```
```{r}
summary(model0.1)
```

```{r}
model0.2 <- lm(sqrt(G3) ~ I((G1 + G2)/2) + absences + famrel + activities + age, data=math_final_grade) 
autoplot(model0.2, which = 1:2, ncol = 2)
```

```{r}
summary(model0.2)
```






```{r}
#This function is used to check the correlation between G3 and other categorical variables
anova_test <- function(math_final_grade, target = "G3") {
  results <- data.frame(
    variable = character(),
    p_value = numeric(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  for (var in names(math_final_grade)) {
    if (is.factor(math_final_grade[[var]]) || is.character(math_final_grade[[var]])) {
      formula <- as.formula(paste(target, "~", var))
      fit <- aov(formula, data = math_final_grade)
      p_val <- summary(fit)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(variable = var, p_value = p_val))
    }
  }
  
  results <- results[order(results$p_value), ]
  return(results)
}

anova_results <- anova_test(math_final_grade, "G3")

anova_results$p_adjusted <- p.adjust(anova_results$p_value, method = "BH")
anova_results[anova_results$p_adjusted < 0.1, ]
```


```{r}

library(dplyr)
num_df <- math_final_grade |> select(where(is.numeric))
r_with_G3 <- cor(num_df, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")["G3", ]
sort(r_with_G3[ names(r_with_G3) != "G3" ], decreasing = TRUE)  
```


```{r}
model1 <- lm(G3 ~ higher + Mjob + romantic + G2 + G1 + Medu + Fedu + goout + failures + age+absences+Walc+famrel+activities, data=math_final_grade) 
autoplot(model1, which = 1:2, ncol = 2) + labs(title = "Model 1")
```

```{r}
summary(model1)
```


```{r}
model2 <- lm(sqrt(G3) ~ higher + Mjob + romantic + G2 + G1 + Medu + Fedu + goout + failures + age+absences+Walc+famrel+activities, data=math_final_grade) 
autoplot(model2, which = 1:2, ncol = 2)+ labs(title = "Model 2")
```

```{r}
summary(model2)
```

```{r}
library(broom)
library(purrr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

models <- list(model1 = model1, model2 = model2)

criteria_df <- map_df(models, ~{
  tibble(
    AIC = AIC(.x),
    BIC = BIC(.x)
  )
}, .id = "Model") %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(AIC, BIC), names_to = "Criterion", values_to = "Value")

ggplot(criteria_df, aes(x = Model, y = Value, fill = Criterion)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "AIC and BIC Comparison of Models",
       x = "Model", y = "Value") +
  theme_minimal()

```


```{r}
AIC(model1, model2)
BIC(model1, model2)
```
```{r}
cor(math_final_grade[, c("G1", "G2", "G3")])

ggplot(math_final_grade, aes(x = G1, y = G2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "red") +
  theme_minimal()
```



```{r}
model3 <- lm(sqrt(G3) ~ higher + Mjob + romantic + I((G1 + G2)/2) + Medu + Fedu + goout + failures + age+absences+Walc+famrel+activities, data=math_final_grade) 

autoplot(model3, which = 1:2, ncol = 2)
```



```{r}
summary(model3)
```

```{r}
AIC(model3, model2)
BIC(model3, model2)
```


```{r}
step.back.aic = step(model3, 
                     direction = "backward", 
                     trace = FALSE)
summary(step.back.aic)
```


```{r}
autoplot(step.back.aic, which = 1:2, ncol = 2)
```


```{r}
set.seed(2)

cv_lm1 = train(
  G3 ~ higher + Mjob + romantic + G2 + G1 + Medu + Fedu + goout + failures+ age+absences+Walc+famrel+activities,math_final_grade,
  method = "lm",
  trControl = trainControl(
    method = "cv", number = 10,
    verboseIter = FALSE
  )
)

cv_lm2 = train(
  sqrt(G3) ~ higher + Mjob + romantic + G2 + G1 + Medu + Fedu + goout + failures + age+absences+Walc+famrel+activities,math_final_grade,
  method = "lm",
  trControl = trainControl(
    method = "cv", number = 10,
    verboseIter = FALSE
  )
)

cv_lm3 = train(
  sqrt(G3) ~ higher + Mjob + romantic + I((G1 + G2)/2) + Medu + Fedu + goout + failures + age+absences+Walc+famrel+activities,math_final_grade,
  method = "lm",
  trControl = trainControl(
    method = "cv", number = 10,
    verboseIter = FALSE
  )
)

cv_lm_fromfullModel = train(
  sqrt(G3) ~ I((G1 + G2)/2) + absences + famrel + activities + age,math_final_grade,
  method = "lm",
  trControl = trainControl(
    method = "cv", number = 10,
    verboseIter = FALSE
  )
)



results <- resamples(list(Model1=cv_lm1, Model2=cv_lm2, Model3=cv_lm3 ,Model4=cv_lm_fromfullModel))

summary(results)
bwplot(results)
```
```{r}
AIC(model0.2 , step.back.aic)
BIC(model0.2 , step.back.aic)
```

```{r}
math_final_grade$pass <- ifelse(math_final_grade$G3 > 0, 1, 0)

model_logit <- glm(pass ~ I((G1 + G2)/2) + absences + Walc + famrel + romantic + activities + age,
                   data = math_final_grade,
                   family = binomial)

summary(model_logit)
```


```{r}
filtered_data <- math_final_grade[math_final_grade$G3 > 0, ]
```


```{r}
num_df <- filtered_data |> select(where(is.numeric))
r_with_G3 <- cor(num_df, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")["G3", ]
sort(r_with_G3[ names(r_with_G3) != "G3" ], decreasing = TRUE)  
```


```{r}
#This function is used to check the correlation between G3 and other categorical variables
anova_test <- function(filtered_data, target = "G3") {
  results <- data.frame(
    variable = character(),
    p_value = numeric(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  for (var in names(filtered_data)) {
    if (is.factor(filtered_data[[var]]) || is.character(filtered_data[[var]])) {
      formula <- as.formula(paste(target, "~", var))
      fit <- aov(formula, data = filtered_data)
      p_val <- summary(fit)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(variable = var, p_value = p_val))
    }
  }
  
  results <- results[order(results$p_value), ]
  return(results)
}

anova_results <- anova_test(filtered_data, "G3")

anova_results$p_adjusted <- p.adjust(anova_results$p_value, method = "BH")
anova_results[anova_results$p_adjusted < 0.1, ]
```

```{r}
refitted_model <- lm(G3 ~ G2 + G1 + Walc + age + famrel + Medu + Fedu + goout + absences + failures + schoolsup + address , data = filtered_data)

summary(refitted_model)
```

```{r}
autoplot(refitted_model, which = 1:2, ncol = 2)
```

```{r}
refitted_model1 <- lm(sqrt(G3) ~ G1 + Walc + age + famrel + Medu + Fedu + goout + absences + failures + schoolsup + address , data = filtered_data)
```


```{r}
autoplot(refitted_model1, which = 1:2, ncol = 2)
```


```{r}
step.back.aic2 = step(refitted_model1, 
                     direction = "backward", 
                     trace = FALSE)
summary(step.back.aic2)
```


```{r}
autoplot(step.back.aic2, which = 1:2, ncol = 2)
```
